name: Stockholm (Sweden)

colors.symbolism: the azure field of the historic municipal arms
the gold (or) of the head of Saint Erik

description: A blue field charged at the centre with the gold crowned head of Saint Erik — the historic coat of arms of the City of Stockholm.
history: The crowned head of Saint Erik has been used as Stockholm's emblem since the 14th century; the seal bearing the design was confirmed by King Magnus Eriksson in 1376. The blue-and-gold flag in its current form is regulated by the city's heraldic statutes; the coat of arms was last formally registered with the Swedish Patent and Registration Office in 1936.

features.symbolism: the blue field of the city's medieval coat of arms

features.role: Coat of arms of Stockholm — the crowned head of Saint Erik
features.symbolism: The crowned, bearded head of Saint Erik (King Erik IX, killed in 1160 and venerated as patron saint of Sweden and of Stockholm), facing the viewer; granted to the city by King Magnus Eriksson in 1376.
